children.heading: Construction
children.label: 1 Construction
children.text: The term “construction” means the installation of infrastructure and the upgrading of existing facilities in locations in which the infrastructure or facilities are associated with the new infrastructure of a rural water project recommended by the Secretary pursuant to this subchapter.

children.heading: Federal reclamation law
children.label: 2 Federal reclamation law
children.text: The term “Federal reclamation law” means the Act of (, chapter 1093), and Acts supplemental to and amendatory of that Act ( et seq.).June 17, 190232 Stat. 38843 U.S.C. 371

children.heading: Indian
children.label: 3 Indian
children.text: The term “Indian” means an individual who is a member of an Indian tribe.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 4 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Non-Federal project entity
children.label: 5 Non-Federal project entity
children.text: The term “non-Federal project entity” means a State, regional, or local authority, Indian tribe or tribal organization, or other qualifying entity, such as a water conservation district, water conservancy district, or rural water district or association.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “operations, maintenance, and replacement costs” means all costs for the operation of a rural water supply project that are necessary for the safe, efficient, and continued functioning of the project to produce the benefits described in a feasibility study.

children.label: i
children.text: repairs of a routine nature that maintain a rural water supply project in a well kept condition;

children.label: ii
children.text: replacement of worn-out project elements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: rehabilitation activities necessary to bring a deteriorated project back to the original condition of the project.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: C Exclusion
children.text: The term “operations, maintenance, and replacement costs” does not include construction costs.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 7 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the rural water supply program carried out under .section 2402 of this title

children.heading: Reclamation States
children.label: 8 Reclamation States
children.text: The term “Reclamation States” means the States and areas referred to in the first section of the Act of ().June 17, 190243 U.S.C. 391

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “rural water supply project” means a project that is designed to serve a community or group of communities, each of which has a population of not more than 50,000 inhabitants, which may include Indian tribes and tribal organizations, dispersed homesites, or rural areas with domestic, industrial, municipal, and residential water.

children.label: i
children.text: incidental noncommercial livestock watering and noncommercial irrigation of vegetation and small gardens of less than 1 acre; and

children.label: I
children.text: pumps, pipes, wells, and other diversions;

children.label: II
children.text: storage tanks and small impoundments;

children.label: III
children.text: water treatment facilities for potable water supplies, including desalination facilities;

children.label: IV
children.text: equipment and management tools for water conservation, groundwater recovery, and water recycling; and

children.label: V
children.text: appurtenances.

children.label: i
children.text: commercial irrigation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: major impoundment structures.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 10 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: the recognized governing body of an Indian tribe; and

children.label: B
children.text: any legally established organization of Indians that is controlled, sanctioned, or chartered by the governing body or democratically elected by the adult members of the Indian community to be served by the organization.
